what_it_does: Claude Code project settings file: disables worktree background isolation and registers one SessionStart hook that runs a bash registry-freshness-check script.
observations: Single SessionStart hook invoking ops/hooks/cc_registry_freshness_check.sh via absolute path
standouts: SessionStart hook ties every Claude Code session opening to a registry-freshness check
conventions: Uses Claude Code's settings.json hooks schema (SessionStart -> hooks -> command)
Hardcoded absolute path /home/tim/company/... rather than a relative or env-based path
concerns: Absolute machine-specific path makes this non-portable across checkouts/machines
notes: If briefing someone: this is the repo's Claude Code config — its one job is firing a registry-freshness check at session start.
questions: What does cc_registry_freshness_check.sh actually verify and what does it do on staleness?
purpose_vs_actual: n/a — config file, no stated purpose to compare; content is self-evident
intention_signals: Wants every session to begin by confirming the live registry is up to date, enforcing the repo's self-description discipline automatically.
novelty: Binds session start to a freshness-check rather than typical lint/format hooks.
contribution: Contributes the wiring that auto-runs a freshness check on session start and the choice to disable worktree bg isolation.
summary_for_embedding: Claude Code project settings.json disabling worktree background isolation and registering a SessionStart command hook that runs a registry-freshness-check bash script.
intention_for_embedding: To automatically verify registry/self-model freshness whenever a Claude Code session begins in this repo.
